contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 45
- Description: DApp permissions — Phase 2 (write path) end-to-end + Edgar deploy
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/75
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/76
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/77
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/253
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/255
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/sentiment_importer/pull/1043
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/191
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dao_client/pull/12
  
  Details:
  ## What changed
  
  Shipped Phase 2 of the operator-proposed DApp permissions module end-to-end. Phase 1 (read-only viewer at `governor_permissions.html`) shipped in an earlier session as `dapp#190`; this session built and deployed the full write path so governors can edit `treasury-cache/permissions.json` from the DApp browser UI or the CLI without touching a PR.
  
  ### Spec (agentic_ai_context)
  
  - New `DAPP_PERMISSION_CHANGE_FLOW.md` documenting the end-to-end flow (DApp → Edgar → GAS → sheet + GitHub), `[DAPP PERMISSION CHANGE EVENT]` payload format, `Dapp Permission Changes` audit-tab schema (cols A–N), GAS handler design (independent governor verification, optimistic concurrency, idempotency on Telegram Update ID), security model, and future enhancements (N-of-M sign-off, sensitivity tiers, public-role friction, time-bounded changes). README index entry added.
  - Two follow-up doc commits: drop the `?secret=` parameter from the spec since Edgar's `WebhookTriggerWorker` only forwards `action`; mark Phase 2 complete and add the CLI alternative subsection.
  
  ### GAS handler (tokenomics)
  
  - New `dapp_permission_change_handler.gs` in `tdg_identity_management/`. Scans Telegram Chat Logs for `[DAPP PERMISSION CHANGE EVENT]` rows whose Telegram Update ID isn't already terminal in the `Dapp Permission Changes` tab. For each: parse, independently verify signer is on the Governors tab (does NOT trust Edgar's pass-through alone), optimistic-concurrency-check roles-before + manifest schema version, mutate `required_roles` in-memory preserving all other manifest fields, PUT to `treasury-cache/permissions.json` via the existing `commitJsonToGithub_` helper, append audit row with status / commit SHA / commit URL. Auto-creates the `Dapp Permission Changes` tab + header on first write. Reuses `CONTRIBUTORS_CACHE_GITHUB_PAT` (already scoped to treasury-cache).
  - Added a third `doGet` routing branch (`?action=apply_permission_change`) to `edgar_send_email_verification.gs` so the same Apps Script project handles all three identity-management ops (sendEmailVerification, refresh_dao_members_cache, apply_permission_change).
  - Followup commit removed the secret-check on the new handler since `WebhookTriggerWorker` only forwards `action` (matches every other dispatch handler in the project — `processTelegramChatLogs`, `parseAndProcessTelegramLogs`, etc.). Real auth is the per-event RSA signature + Governors-tab membership; deployment URL is the unguessable access token.
  - Deployed twice via `clasp push` to script ID `1m8IZPs1vFN99cuu-39kbC-OGXggRVtJtXq5rfSB0M1sCQjMdolEUDuGU`.
  
  ### Edgar dispatch (sentiment_importer)
  
  - New `config.dapp_permission_change_webhook_url` setting in `application.rb`. Default points at the existing tdg_identity_management deployment URL (same project handles the new doGet branch). Override via `DAPP_PERMISSION_CHANGE_WEBHOOK_URL` env var.
  - New `elsif text.include?('[DAPP PERMISSION CHANGE EVENT]')` branch in `dao_controller.rb`. Enqueues via the existing `WebhookTriggerWorker` pattern (HTTParty GET with `?action=apply_permission_change`). Background; does not block the original signed POST. Graceful warning if the config URL is unset.
  - **Production deploy via `./deploy.sh`** — both `seni_ror` (Rails app, port 3002) and `seni_sk` (Sidekiq worker) restarted; verified `edgar.truesight.me/ping` → 200 OK in ~380 ms.
  
  ### DApp edit mode (dapp)
  
  - Rewrote `governor_permissions.html` from a read-only viewer into a viewer + editor. Edit-mode toggle pill replaces the static role-badge row with interactive role-toggle chips (`governor` / `member` / `operator` / `custodian` / `public`). Adding `public` triggers an explicit confirm() prompt — the security model recommends deliberate friction since public-callable actions are a meaningful expansion. Per-card change tracking with amber "modified" pill + `was: [...]` hint. Pending-changes panel summarizes diffs as readable bullets. "Submit N change(s)" button signs one `[DAPP PERMISSION CHANGE EVENT]` per pending change and POSTs to `EDGAR_SUBMIT` sequentially with per-change result lines (✔ applied / ✖ error). After submission, waits 8 s for GAS to drain its queue, re-fetches manifest + snapshot, re-renders so the page reflects post-apply state. Direct link to the `Dapp Permission Changes` tab for the audit row. menu.js cache-busted ?v= 20260429 → 20260430 across all 27 HTML pages + `service-worker.js` `URLS_TO_CACHE`.
  
  ### CLI (dao_client)
  
  - New `report_dapp_permission_change.py` module — terminal CLI parallel to the DApp UI. Same signed event format, same Edgar pipeline, same audit row. Auto-resolves `--roles-before` and `--schema-version` from the live `permissions.json` via raw URL if omitted (avoids stale-state copy-paste errors). No-op short-circuit if before == after. Action-not-found errors surface the known actions list. `Submission Source` records `dao_client://truesight_dao_client.modules.report_dapp_permission_change` so the audit trail tells you whether the change came from the browser or the terminal. Console-script entry registered (`truesight-dao-report-dapp-permission-change`). README modules table updated.
  
  ## Why
  
  Operator framed Phase 2 in two earlier turns: "DApp should send to the same Edgar endpoint … the appropriate GAS script receives a doGet request if Edgar detects that there is such a matching event and the GAS should add a new row to the 'Dapp Permission Changes' [tab] and then do a Github update of the permissions." This session implemented exactly that loop, with three safety properties woven in:
  
  1. **Three audit surfaces fire simultaneously on every applied change** — Telegram Chat Logs row (universal event ledger), Dapp Permission Changes row (per-change with status + commit reference), git commit history on `treasury-cache/permissions.json`. No single layer can quietly hide a change.
  2. **Independent governor re-verification inside the GAS handler** — the GAS does not trust Edgar's pass-through alone; it re-resolves the signer's public key and re-checks Governors-tab membership at processing time. Defense-in-depth.
  3. **Optimistic concurrency on roles-before + schema version** — two governors editing concurrently can't silently clobber each other; whoever commits second gets `status=concurrency_conflict` and re-edits from a fresh viewer load.
  
  End-to-end loop is live as of the Edgar deploy at the end of this session.
  
  ## Pull requests
  
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/75
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/76
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/77
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/253
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/255
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/sentiment_importer/pull/1043
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp/pull/191
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dao_client/pull/12
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
- TDG Issued: 75.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
